title: Phase 2: thread worktree path through ToolContext (followup #345)
description:
Followup to #345.

Phase 1 (landed in #345): execute_enter_worktree and execute_exit_worktree no longer mutate process CWD. enter_worktree returns the new worktree path in its success message; exit_worktree requires an explicit 'path' argument. All git invocations use Command::current_dir(cwd) -- no subprocess depends on the parent process CWD either.

Phase 2 (this issue):
- Extend ToolContext with cwd: Option<&Path> populated from the session's active WorktreeState.
- Update bash/file/lsp tool handlers to use ctx.cwd when resolving relative paths and launching subprocesses (Command::current_dir).
- ChatSession should grow an active_worktree: Option<WorktreeState> field, and the REPL should parse the path out of enter_worktree's response (or better, have enter_worktree return structured data) to populate it.
- Add WorktreeGuard / Drop-based cleanup so session crashes don't leak worktrees on disk.

Acceptance:
- A bash call following enter_worktree resolves relative paths against the worktree, not the process CWD.
- Concurrent tool calls in different sessions never observe each other's worktree paths.
